title: Oden’s Past Begins to Define Wano’s Tragedy
description: On pages 14–15, this part focuses on Unknown person #13, Unknown person #14, Hyogoro, Denjiro, Kinemon, Oden as The story turns to Oden’s past, showing how his life, Whitebeard, Roger, and Orochi shaped Wano’s tragedy.
scene: The story turns to Oden’s past, showing how his life, Whitebeard, Roger, and Orochi shaped Wano’s tragedy.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #13
dialogue.text: Huh... is that how it is!?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: What is with that guy? He is so... cool..

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: This means trouble!! Hide your wives and daughters!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: So this is the charming open from the rumours!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #13
dialogue.text: Did you guys come to capture me?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Yes, well... I did end up being bribed, but...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You should not talk about such things out loud, you idiot.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: ....Anyway, what is going on here? No one is avoiding me like they usually do.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The fire bell has been ringing nonstop... but there seems to be no fire nearby...?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #13
dialogue.text: The following

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #13
dialogue.text: Hey, is it a big fire!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #13
dialogue.text: It is bigger...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #13
dialogue.text: than a mere fire...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: How could you not know about this, Kin'Emon!?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You should never have brought THAT with you!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #14
dialogue.text: Huh?...you mean the White Board?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #14
dialogue.text: Boss Korokoma probably wanted to use it...

dialogue.speaker: Hyogoro
dialogue.text: To crush the Hyogorougoro family!

dialogue.speaker: Denjiro
dialogue.text: What do you mean, Denjiro?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Like I said earlier... wherever that little boar goes...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #14
dialogue.text: Its parent is not far behind.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: According to the ancient texts, a full-grown white boar is the size of a mountain!! Many refer to them as MOUNTAIN GODS!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It is said that hundreds of years ago, a whole region was annillated in a single night.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I thought that it was just a fairy tale, but if... this WAITE BOAR is here now... then that means...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #14
dialogue.text: Clang Cang Clang Clang!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #14
dialogue.text: A mountain gooooood!!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #14
dialogue.text: A real mountain God!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: We are going to be eaten alive!! There are already many victims!!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #14
dialogue.text: EVACUATE THE CAPITALL!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #14
dialogue.text: Oink!! Oink!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #14
dialogue.text: I was right... it really showed up...!!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Oh, crap... Will I be responsible for the destruction of the Capital!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #14
dialogue.text: That is an interesting story to overhear.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #14
dialogue.text: I may not know you two, but....!!

dialogue.speaker: Kinemon
dialogue.text: KC... Ko-ko- Kozuki Oden!!?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I WANT YOU TO GIVE ME THAT BOAR!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #14
dialogue.text: An unprecedented incident!!

summary: This section advances the Wano - Oden and the Old Era storyline with 37 dialogue beats. The story turns to Oden’s past, showing how his life, Whitebeard, Roger, and Orochi shaped Wano’s tragedy.
